dimensions.rationale: The hash-bound receipt is LOCAL_SYNTHETIC_ONLY with zero remote writes and no protected execution; closure protection covers all workflow paths and the reviewed effect/evidence surfaces.

The verified receipt digest binds the exact baseline/candidate/tree and records the complete unique 15-command local gate set with exit-zero results and sanitized-output digests; v2 provenance enforces ordered distinct task/model/reply bindings.
The receipt expressly reports no protected execution and no sensitive data, while structured receipt scanning independently blocks the specified secret patterns; no production conclusion is inferred.

The reviewed execution reports no external writes and removed ephemeral outputs; the drift regression proves later production-workflow or capture-tool changes block closure while documentation-only changes do not.

findings.finding: PRODUCTION_RUN is capacity-gated and KILL-007 disables production_enabled, removing the prior UNKNOWN/RED capacity and Kill Criterion bypass.
findings.resolution_ref: OperationalGate applies both KILL-007 and fail-closed capacity checks to PRODUCTION_RUN before protected composition proceeds.

findings.finding: Post-review closure now protects every workflow plus the capture and assurance surfaces, and regression coverage rejects later production-workflow or evidence-capture drift.
findings.resolution_ref: The protected prefix set includes .github/workflows/ and machine/tools/capture_candidate_gates.py; the adversarial temporary-Git tests verify blocking behavior.

limitations: Static read-only re-review only; no tests or validators were executed.
This does not prove remote CI, protected Oracle behavior, real Gmail/private-repository behavior, production health, final AC-033, deployment, publication, or RMD-06/RMD-07 readiness.
Repository hashes and task IDs remain auditable as platform execution identities only through the retained Codex thread audit log.
summary: PASS: the immutable candidate and hash-bound local synthetic receipt resolve the Terra original and adverse findings, with no open finding in the reviewed scope.
